.PHONY: verify check lint test-imports lint-fix

verify:
	@echo "Running build verification..."
	@uv run python core/utils/scripts/verify_build.py

check: verify

lint:
	@echo "Running ruff linter..."
	@uv run ruff check core/

lint-fix:
	@echo "Running ruff linter with auto-fix..."
	@uv run ruff check --fix core/

test-imports:
	@echo "Testing critical imports..."
	@uv run python core/utils/scripts/check_imports.py

## Install optional BIM packages (ifcopenshell, etc.)
install-bim:
	uv pip install ifcopenshell numpy networkx ladybug-core

## Run BIM-specific tests
test-bim:
	uv run python -m pytest tests/test_bim_tools.py -v

<sub>⏱️ 2m 36s</sub>

### ✅ `view`

**view the file at /teamspace/studios/this_studio/suna-bim-agent/apps/mobile/.cursorrules.**

/teamspace/studios/this_studio/suna-bim-agent/apps/mobile/.cursorrules

<details>
<summary>738 lines</summary>

````
1. # Kortix Project - Development Rules
2. 
3. ## Project Overview
4. Kortix is a React Native + Expo app built with TypeScript, NativeWind (Tailwind CSS), and a custom design system.
5. 
6. ## Core Technologies
7. - **Framework**: React Native (Expo)
8. - **Styling**: NativeWind (Tailwind CSS for React Native)
9. - **Font**: Roobert (custom font family)
10. - **Icons**: Lucide React Native
11. - **Animations**: React Native Reanimated
12. - **Routing**: Expo Router
13. - **Type Safety**: TypeScript (strict mode)
14. 
15. ---
16. 
17. ## 1. CODE ORGANIZATION
18. 
19. ### File Structure
20. ```
21. /app                    # Expo Router screens
22. /components
23.   /home                 # Home screen components
24.   /input                # Input & agent system components
25.   /ui                   # Reusable UI primitives
26. /hooks                  # Custom React hooks
27. /lib                    # Utility functions & configurations
28. /assets
29.   /brand                # Brand assets (logos, symbols)
30.   /font/Roobert         # Custom font files
31. ```
32. 
33. ### Component Organization Rules
34. 
35. 1. **One component per file** - Never combine multiple components in a single file
36. 2. **Co-locate related components** - Group by feature, not by type
37. 3. **Create index.ts files** - For clean imports from component folders
38. 4. **Separate concerns** - Logic (hooks) vs Presentation (components)
39. 
40. Example:
41. ```tsx
42. // ✅ GOOD
43. /components/input/
44.   - ChatInput.tsx
45.   - AgentSelector.tsx
46.   - AgentDrawer.tsx
47.   - AgentAvatar.tsx
48.   - types.ts
49.   - agents.ts
50.   - index.ts
51. 
52. // ❌ BAD
53. /components/
54.   - AllChatComponents.tsx
55.   - types.ts
56. ```
57. 
58. ---
59. 
60. ## 2. COLOR SYSTEM (CRITICAL)
61. 
62. ### STRICT RULE: Use Design Tokens ONLY
63. 
64. **NEVER use hardcoded colors**. Always use semantic tokens from `global.css`.
65. 
66. ### Color Token Reference
67. ```tsx
68. // Background colors
69. bg-background          // Main app background (#F8F8F8 light / #121215 dark)
70. bg-card                // Card surfaces (#FFFFFF light / #161618 dark)
71. bg-popover             // Popover backgrounds
72. bg-input               // Input backgrounds
73. 
74. // Text colors
75. text-foreground        // Primary text (#121215 light / #F8F8F8 dark)
76. text-card-foreground   // Card text
77. text-muted             // Muted/secondary text
78. text-muted-foreground  // Even more muted
79. 
80. // Interactive colors
81. bg-primary             // Primary buttons/elements
82. bg-secondary           // Secondary surfaces
83. bg-accent              // Accent colors
84. bg-destructive         // Destructive actions
85. 
86. // Borders
87. border-border          // Standard borders
88. border-input           // Input borders
89. 
90. // Utility
91. bg-primary/10          // 10% opacity variants (use /5, /10, /15, /20, etc.)
92. text-foreground/60     // 60% opacity text
93. ```
94. 
95. ### Examples
96. 
97. ```tsx
98. // ✅ GOOD - Using design tokens
99. <View className="bg-background">
100.   <View className="bg-card border border-border">
101.     <Text className="text-foreground">Title</Text>
102.     <Text className="text-muted">Subtitle</Text>
103.   </View>
104. </View>
105. 
106. // ❌ BAD - Hardcoded colors
107. <View className="bg-[#F8F8F8]">
108.   <View style={{ backgroundColor: '#FFFFFF' }}>
109.     <Text className="text-[#121215]">Title</Text>
110.   </View>
111. </View>
112. 
113. // ✅ GOOD - Opacity modifiers
114. <View className="bg-primary/10">
115. <Text className="text-foreground/60">
116. 
117. // ❌ BAD - Inline rgba
118. <View style={{ backgroundColor: 'rgba(18, 18, 21, 0.1)' }}>
119. ```
120. 
121. ### TextInput placeholderTextColor
122. For TextInput components, use HSL format:
123. ```tsx
124. <TextInput
125.   placeholderTextColor="hsl(var(--foreground))"
126.   className="text-foreground"
127. />
128. ```
129. 
130. ---
131. 
132. ## 3. FONT SYSTEM
133. 
134. ### Font Configuration
135. The app uses **Roobert** font family exclusively.
136. 
137. ### Font Weight Classes
138. ```tsx
139. font-roobert           // Regular (400)
140. font-roobert-light     // Light (300)
141. font-roobert-medium    // Medium (500)
142. font-roobert-semibold  // Semi-bold (600)
143. font-roobert-bold      // Bold (700)
144. font-roobert-heavy     // Heavy (800)
145. ```
146. 
147. ### Usage Rules
148. 
149. 1. **Use Tailwind classes** for UI components:
150. ```tsx
151. <Text className="text-base font-roobert-medium">Medium Text</Text>
152. <Text className="text-xl font-roobert-semibold">Heading</Text>
153. ```
154. 
155. 2. **Use style prop** for TextInput:
156. ```tsx
157. <TextInput
158.   style={{ fontFamily: 'Roobert-Regular' }}
159.   className="text-foreground text-[15px]"
160. />
161. ```
162. 
163. 3. **Default weights**:
164.    - Body text: `font-roobert` (Regular)
165.    - UI elements: `font-roobert-medium`
166.    - Headings: `font-roobert-semibold` or `font-roobert-bold`
167. 
168. ---
169. 
170. ## 4. COMPONENT PATTERNS
171. 
172. ### Custom Hooks Pattern
173. 
174. **ALWAYS extract logic into custom hooks**. Keep components clean and presentational.
175. 
176. ```tsx
177. // ✅ GOOD - Logic in custom hooks
178. // hooks/useAgentManager.ts
179. export function useAgentManager() {
180.   const [selectedAgent, setSelectedAgent] = useState(DEFAULT_AGENT);
181.   const [isDrawerVisible, setIsDrawerVisible] = useState(false);
182.   
183.   const openDrawer = () => setIsDrawerVisible(true);
184.   const closeDrawer = () => setIsDrawerVisible(false);
185.   const selectAgent = (agent: Agent) => {
186.     setSelectedAgent(agent);
187.     console.log('Agent selected:', agent);
188.   };
189.   
190.   return {
191.     selectedAgent,
192.     isDrawerVisible,
193.     openDrawer,
194.     closeDrawer,
195.     selectAgent,
196.     agents: AGENTS,
197.   };
198. }
199. 
200. // app/index.tsx
201. export default function HomeScreen() {
202.   const agentManager = useAgentManager();
203.   
204.   return (
205.     <ChatInput onAgentPress={agentManager.openDrawer} />
206.   );
207. }
208. 
209. // ❌ BAD - Logic mixed with presentation
210. export default function HomeScreen() {
211.   const [selectedAgent, setSelectedAgent] = useState(DEFAULT_AGENT);
212.   const [isDrawerVisible, setIsDrawerVisible] = useState(false);
213.   // ... 50 more lines of logic
214.   
215.   return <View>...</View>;
216. }
217. ```
218. 
219. ### Component Structure
220. 
221. ```tsx
222. // 1. Imports (grouped)
223. import { Text } from '@/components/ui/text';
224. import { Icon } from '@/components/ui/icon';
225. import * as React from 'react';
226. import { Pressable, View } from 'react-native';
227. import { Menu } from 'lucide-react-native';
228. 
229. // 2. Types/Interfaces
230. interface TopNavProps {
231.   onMenuPress?: () => void;
232.   currentCredits?: number;
233. }
234. 
235. // 3. Component (with JSDoc)
236. /**
237.  * Top Navigation Bar Component
238.  * 
239.  * Displays menu icon, theme switcher, and credits.
240.  */
241. export function TopNav({ onMenuPress, currentCredits = 250 }: TopNavProps) {
242.   // 4. Hooks
243.   const { colorScheme } = useColorScheme();
244.   
245.   // 5. Event handlers
246.   const handlePress = () => {
247.     console.log('Menu pressed');
248.     onMenuPress?.();
249.   };
250.   
251.   // 6. Render
252.   return (
253.     <View className="flex-row items-center">
254.       <Pressable onPress={handlePress}>
255.         <Icon as={Menu} size={24} className="text-foreground" />
256.       </Pressable>
257.     </View>
258.   );
259. }
260. ```
261. 
262. ---
263. 
264. ## 5. ANIMATIONS
265. 
266. ### Use React Native Reanimated
267. 
268. ```tsx
269. import Animated, { 
270.   useAnimatedStyle, 
271.   useSharedValue, 
272.   withSpring 
273. } from 'react-native-reanimated';
274. 
275. const AnimatedPressable = Animated.createAnimatedComponent(Pressable);
276. 
277. export function AnimatedButton() {
278.   const scale = useSharedValue(1);
279.   
280.   const animatedStyle = useAnimatedStyle(() => ({
281.     transform: [{ scale: scale.value }],
282.   }));
283.   
284.   return (
285.     <AnimatedPressable
286.       onPressIn={() => {
287.         scale.value = withSpring(0.9, { damping: 15, stiffness: 400 });
288.       }}
289.       onPressOut={() => {
290.         scale.value = withSpring(1, { damping: 15, stiffness: 400 });
291.       }}
292.       style={animatedStyle}
293.     >
294.       {/* ... */}
295.     </AnimatedPressable>
296.   );
297. }
298. ```
299. 
300. ### Animation Config
301. - **Spring animations**: `{ damping: 15, stiffness: 400 }` for quick, responsive feel
302. - **Duration**: 200-300ms for most transitions
303. - **Easing**: Spring for natural feel, avoid linear
304. 
305. ---
306. 
307. ## 6. ICONS
308. 
309. ### ALWAYS Use Lucide React Native
310. 
311. ```tsx
312. import { Menu, Zap, Plus, Moon, Sun } from 'lucide-react-native';
313. 
314. // ✅ GOOD - Lucide with proper theming
315. <Icon 
316.   as={Menu}
317.   size={24}
318.   className="text-foreground"
319. />
320. 
321. // ❌ BAD - PNG images
322. <Image source={require('./menu-icon.png')} />
323. ```
324. 
325. ### Icon Guidelines
326. - Use `Icon` wrapper component from `@/components/ui/icon`
327. - Size: 16-24px for UI, 32px+ for feature icons
328. - Color: Always use `text-foreground` or other semantic tokens
329. - strokeWidth: 2 (default) or specified
330. 
331. ---
332. 
333. ## 7. CONSOLE LOGGING
334. 
335. **ALWAYS log user interactions** for debugging.
336. 
337. ```tsx
338. const handlePress = () => {
339.   console.log('🎯 Action:', 'Menu pressed');
340.   console.log('⏰ Timestamp:', new Date().toISOString());
341.   console.log('📊 Data:', { userId, screen: 'Home' });
342.   onPress?.();
343. };
344. ```
345. 
346. ### Emoji Convention
347. - 🎯 User actions
348. - 🤖 Agent/AI operations
349. - 📳 Haptic feedback
350. - ⏰ Timestamps
351. - 📊 Data objects
352. - ✅ Success
353. - ❌ Errors
354. - 🌓 Theme changes
355. 
356. ---
357. 
358. ## 8. THEME SYSTEM
359. 
360. ### Light Mode First
361. The app is built **light mode first**, with dark mode as a variant.
362. 
363. ```tsx
364. // Access theme
365. import { useColorScheme } from 'nativewind';
366. 
367. const { colorScheme, toggleColorScheme } = useColorScheme();
368. 
369. // Check theme
370. if (colorScheme === 'dark') {
371.   // Dark mode specific logic
372. }
373. 
374. // Theme-aware components
375. const SymbolComponent = colorScheme === 'dark' ? WhiteSymbol : BlackSymbol;
376. ```
377. 
378. ### Theme-Aware Assets
379. ```tsx
380. // ✅ GOOD - Theme-aware
381. export function BackgroundLogo() {
382.   const { colorScheme } = useColorScheme();
383.   const Symbol = colorScheme === 'dark' ? SymbolWhite : SymbolBlack;
384.   return <Symbol />;
385. }
386. 
387. // ❌ BAD - Static asset
388. export function BackgroundLogo() {
389.   return <Symbol />;
390. }
391. ```
392. 
393. ---
394. 
395. ## 9. TYPESCRIPT RULES
396. 
397. ### Type Safety
398. ```tsx
399. // ✅ GOOD - Explicit types
400. interface Agent {
401.   id: string;
402.   name: string;
403.   icon: LucideIcon;
404.   description?: string;
405. }
406. 
407. export function AgentDrawer({ agents }: { agents: Agent[] }) {
408.   // ...
409. }
410. 
411. // ❌ BAD - Any types
412. export function AgentDrawer({ agents }: { agents: any[] }) {
413.   // ...
414. }
415. ```
416. 
417. ### Type Organization
418. - Create `types.ts` files in component folders
419. - Export types alongside components
420. - Use interfaces for objects, types for unions
421. 
422. ---
423. 
424. ## 10. STYLING RULES
425. 
426. ### NativeWind Best Practices
427. 
428. ```tsx
429. // ✅ GOOD - Tailwind classes
430. <View className="flex-1 bg-background px-6">
431.   <Text className="text-lg font-roobert-semibold text-foreground">
432.     Title
433.   </Text>
434. </View>
435. 
436. // ✅ GOOD - Style prop for dimensions
437. <View 
438.   className="bg-card rounded-2xl"
439.   style={{ width: 33.75, height: 33.75 }}
440. >
441. 
442. // ❌ BAD - Inline styles for colors
443. <View style={{ backgroundColor: '#F8F8F8', padding: 24 }}>
444.   <Text style={{ color: '#121215', fontSize: 18 }}>
445.     Title
446.   </Text>
447. </View>
448. ```
449. 
450. ### Spacing Scale
451. **ALWAYS use Tailwind's standardized spacing scale** - Never use custom pixel values.
452. 
453. ```tsx
454. // ✅ GOOD - Tailwind spacing
455. <View className="gap-3 p-4 mx-6 mb-8">
456. <View className="gap-1">  // 4px
457. <View className="gap-2">  // 8px
458. <View className="gap-3">  // 12px
459. <View className="gap-4">  // 16px
460. 
461. // ❌ BAD - Custom spacing
462. <View style={{ gap: 11.25 }}>
463. <View style={{ padding: 15.5 }}>
464. ```
465. 
466. **Tailwind Spacing Reference:**
467. - `gap-1` to `gap-12` (4px to 48px in 4px increments)
468. - `p-2`, `px-4`, `py-3`, etc. (padding)
469. - `m-2`, `mx-3`, `mb-8`, etc. (margin)
470. - Use `gap-x` and `gap-y` for directional gaps
471. 
472. ---
473. 
474. ## 11. IMPORT ORGANIZATION
475. 
476. ### Import Order
477. ```tsx
478. // 1. UI Components
479. import { Text } from '@/components/ui/text';
480. import { Icon } from '@/components/ui/icon';
481. 
482. // 2. Feature Components
483. import { AgentDrawer, ChatInput } from '@/components/input';
484. import { TopNav, BackgroundLogo } from '@/components/home';
485. 
486. // 3. Hooks
487. import { useAgentManager, useChatInput } from '@/hooks';
488. 
489. // 4. External Libraries
490. import { Stack } from 'expo-router';
491. import { useColorScheme } from 'nativewind';
492. 
493. // 5. React
494. import * as React from 'react';
495. 
496. // 6. React Native
497. import { View, Pressable, TextInput } from 'react-native';
498. 
499. // 7. Icons
500. import { Menu, Zap } from 'lucide-react-native';
501. 
502. // 8. Types
503. import type { Agent } from './types';
504. ```
505. 
506. ### Path Aliases
507. ```tsx
508. // ✅ GOOD - Use @ alias
509. import { Text } from '@/components/ui/text';
510. import { AGENTS } from '@/components/input';
511. 
512. // ❌ BAD - Relative paths
513. import { Text } from '../../components/ui/text';
514. ```
515. 
516. ---
517. 
518. ## 12. PERFORMANCE
519. 
520. ### Optimization Rules
521. 1. **Memoize expensive computations**
522.    ```tsx
523.    const memoizedValue = React.useMemo(() => computeExpensive(), [deps]);
524.    ```
525. 
526. 2. **Use React.memo for pure components**
527.    ```tsx
528.    export const AgentAvatar = React.memo(function AgentAvatar({ agent }) {
529.      // ...
530.    });
531.    ```
532. 
533. 3. **Avoid inline functions in render**
534.    ```tsx
535.    // ✅ GOOD
536.    const handlePress = useCallback(() => {
537.      onPress?.();
538.    }, [onPress]);
539.    
540.    // ❌ BAD
541.    <Pressable onPress={() => onPress?.()} />
542.    ```
543. 
544. ---
545. 
546. ## 13. DOCUMENTATION
547. 
548. ### Component Documentation
549. ```tsx
550. /**
551.  * ChatInput Component - Pixel-perfect recreation from Figma
552.  * Node ID: 181:9659
553.  * 
554.  * Features:
555.  * - Multi-line text input
556.  * - Attach button with animation
557.  * - Agent selector
558.  * - Send button
559.  * 
560.  * Specifications:
561.  * - Height: 120px
562.  * - Border radius: 20.625px
563.  * - Padding: 16px
564.  */
565. export function ChatInput({ ... }) {
566.   // ...
567. }
568. ```
569. 
570. ### README Files
571. Each major component folder should have a `README.md`:
572. - Purpose and overview
573. - Component list
574. - Usage examples
575. - Props documentation
576. 
577. ---
578. 
579. ## 14. GIT WORKFLOW
580. 
581. ### NEVER commit without explicit user request
582. ```tsx
583. // In chat:
584. // ❌ "I've committed the changes"
585. // ✅ "The changes are ready. Would you like me to commit them?"
586. ```
587. 
588. ### Commit Message Format
589. ```
590. feat: Add theme switcher component
591. fix: Resolve keyboard dismissal issue
592. refactor: Extract agent logic into custom hook
593. docs: Update color system documentation
594. ```
595. 
596. ---
597. 
598. ## 15. ERROR HANDLING
599. 
600. ### Graceful Degradation
601. ```tsx
602. // ✅ GOOD - Safe optional chaining
603. onPress?.();
604. agent?.name ?? 'Unknown Agent';
605. 
606. // ✅ GOOD - Error boundaries for crash prevention
607. try {
608.   riskyOperation();
609. } catch (error) {
610.   console.error('❌ Error:', error);
611.   // Fallback UI
612. }
613. ```
614. 
615. ---
616. 
617. ## 16. ACCESSIBILITY
618. 
619. ### Basic Accessibility
620. ```tsx
621. <Pressable
622.   onPress={handlePress}
623.   accessibilityLabel="Open menu"
624.   accessibilityRole="button"
625.   accessibilityHint="Opens the navigation menu"
626. >
627.   <Icon as={Menu} size={24} />
628. </Pressable>
629. ```
630. 
631. ---
632. 
633. ## 17. COMMON PATTERNS
634. 
635. ### Conditional Rendering
636. ```tsx
637. // ✅ GOOD - Optional chaining
638. {onCreateAgent && (
639.   <Pressable onPress={onCreateAgent}>...</Pressable>
640. )}
641. 
642. // ✅ GOOD - Ternary for either/or
643. {isLoading ? <Spinner /> : <Content />}
644. ```
645. 
646. ### List Rendering
647. ```tsx
648. // ✅ GOOD - Proper keys
649. {agents.map((agent) => (
650.   <AgentCard key={agent.id} agent={agent} />
651. ))}
652. 
653. // ❌ BAD - Index as key
654. {agents.map((agent, index) => (
655.   <AgentCard key={index} agent={agent} />
656. ))}
657. ```
658. 
659. ---
660. 
661. ## 18. DO NOT CREATE
662. 
663. - **Helper scripts** - Use standard tools
664. - **Workarounds** - Fix the root cause
665. - **Temporary files** - Clean up after yourself
666. - **Documentation files** - Unless explicitly requested
667. - **Config overrides** - Respect project settings
668. 
669. ---
670. 
671. ## 19. TESTING MINDSET
672. 
673. ### Manual Testing Checklist
674. - [ ] Light mode works
675. - [ ] Dark mode works
676. - [ ] Theme switching is smooth
677. - [ ] All colors use design tokens
678. - [ ] Fonts load correctly
679. - [ ] Icons display properly
680. - [ ] Animations are smooth
681. - [ ] Console logs work
682. - [ ] No linter errors
683. 
684. ---
685. 
686. ## 20. QUICK REFERENCE
687. 
688. ### Most Used Commands
689. ```bash
690. # Start dev server
691. npx expo start --clear
692. 
693. # Check linter
694. # Use read_lints tool
695. 
696. # Install package
697. npm install package-name
698. ```
699. 
700. ### Most Used Patterns
701. ```tsx
702. // Color
703. className="bg-background text-foreground"
704. 
705. // Font
706. className="font-roobert-medium"
707. 
708. // Animation
709. const scale = useSharedValue(1);
710. scale.value = withSpring(0.9);
711. 
712. // Theme
713. const { colorScheme } = useColorScheme();
714. 
715. // Logging
716. console.log('🎯 Action:', 'Button pressed');
717. ```
718. 
719. ---
720. 
721. ## SUMMARY: THE GOLDEN RULES
722. 
723. 1. ✅ **Use design tokens ONLY** - Never hardcode colors
724. 2. ✅ **Use Roobert font** - Never use system fonts
725. 3. ✅ **Extract logic to hooks** - Keep components clean
726. 4. ✅ **One component per file** - Stay organized
727. 5. ✅ **Use Lucide icons** - No PNG images
728. 6. ✅ **Log everything** - Console.log user actions
729. 7. ✅ **TypeScript strict** - No any types
730. 8. ✅ **Theme-aware** - Support light and dark modes
731. 9. ✅ **Animate with Reanimated** - Smooth interactions
732. 10. ✅ **Document well** - Future you will thank you
733. 
734. ---
735. 
736. **Remember**: Clean code is not about being clever, it's about being clear, consistent, and maintainable. When in doubt, follow the patterns already established in the codebase.
737. 
738.
````

</details>

$ cat /teamspace/studios/this_studio/suna-bim-agent/backend/core/agentpress/tool.py 2>/dev/null | head -80 && echo "---" && cat /teamspace/studios/this_studio/suna-bim-agent/backend/core/tools/tool_registry.py 2>/dev/null | head -60

<details>

```
"""
Core tool system providing the foundation for creating and managing tools.

This module defines the base classes and decorators for creating tools:
- Tool base class for implementing tool functionality
- Schema decorators for OpenAPI tool definitions
- Metadata decorators for tool and method information
- Result containers for standardized tool outputs
"""

from typing import Dict, Any, Union, Optional, List
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from abc import ABC
import json
import inspect
from enum import Enum
from core.utils.logger import logger

class SchemaType(Enum):
    """Enumeration of supported schema types for tool definitions."""
    OPENAPI = "openapi"

@dataclass
class ToolSchema:
    """Container for tool schemas with type information.
    
    Attributes:
        schema_type (SchemaType): Type of schema (OpenAPI)
        schema (Dict[str, Any]): The actual schema definition
    """
    schema_type: SchemaType
    schema: Dict[str, Any]

@dataclass
class ToolResult:
    """Container for tool execution results.
    
    Attributes:
        success (bool): Whether the tool execution succeeded
        output (Any): Output data (can be dict, list, or string)
    """
    success: bool
    output: Any
@dataclass
class ToolMetadata:
    """Container for tool-level metadata.
    
    Attributes:
        display_name (str): Human-readable tool name
        description (str): Tool description (short, for minimal index)
        icon (Optional[str]): Icon identifier for UI
        color (Optional[str]): Color class for UI styling
        is_core (bool): Whether this is a core tool (always enabled)
        weight (int): Sort order (lower = higher priority, default 100)
        visible (bool): Whether tool is visible in frontend UI (default False)
        usage_guide (Optional[str]): Detailed usage instructions loaded on-demand
    """
    display_name: str
    description: str
    icon: Optional[str] = None
    color: Optional[str] = None
    is_core: bool = False
    weight: int = 100
    visible: bool = False
    usage_guide: Optional[str] = None

@dataclass
class MethodMetadata:
    """Container for method-level metadata.
    
    Attributes:
        display_name (str): Human-readable method name
        description (str): Method description
        is_core (bool): Whether this is a core method (always enabled)
        visible (bool): Whether method is visible in frontend UI (default True)
    """
    display_name: str
    description: str
    is_core: bool = False
    visible: bool = True
---
from typing import Dict, List, Tuple, Type, Optional
from core.agentpress.tool import Tool

CORE_TOOLS = [
    ('expand_msg_tool', 'core.tools.expand_msg_tool', 'ExpandMessageTool'),
    ('message_tool', 'core.tools.message_tool', 'MessageTool'),
    ('task_list_tool', 'core.tools.task_list_tool', 'TaskListTool'),
    ('sb_git_sync', 'core.tools.sb_git_sync', 'SandboxGitTool'),
]

SANDBOX_TOOLS = [
    ('sb_shell_tool', 'core.tools.sb_shell_tool', 'SandboxShellTool'),

Agent completed. agent_id: explore-codebase, agent_type: explore, status: completed, description: explore-codebase, elapsed: 286s, total_turns: 0, duration: 140s

Perfect! Now I have enough information to provide a comprehensive analysis. Let me compile everything:

## Comprehensive Codebase Analysis: Carbon BIM Agent Platform

---

### **1. OVERALL PROJECT STRUCTURE & PURPOSE**

**Project**: Carbon BIM — An AI agent platform for Building Information Modeling (BIM) analysis  
**Primary Goal**: Provide IFC model processing, carbon calculation, clash detection, and Thai building code compliance checking  
**Tech Stack**:
- **Backend**: Python/FastAPI with LiteLLM (multi-provider LLM support)
- **Frontend**: Next.js 15 with Turbopack, Tailwind CSS 4, Radix UI
- **Mobile**: React Native/Expo with NativeWind (Tailwind CSS)
- **Database**: Supabase (auth, database, storage)
- **Cache/Pub-Sub**: Redis
- **Infrastructure**: Pulumi, Docker Compose, Dokploy

**Monorepo Structure**:
```
├── apps/
│   ├── frontend/       # Next.js 15 dashboard
│   ├── mobile/         # React Native/Expo app
│   └── desktop/        # Desktop application
├── backend/            # Python FastAPI service
│   ├── api.py          # Main entry point
│   ├── core/
│   │   ├── agentpress/     # Agent runtime framework
│   │   ├── agents/         # Agent CRUD, runs, tools APIs
│   │   ├── bim/            # BIM-specific API endpoints
│   │   ├── tools/          # Agent tool implementations
│   │   │   └── bim/        # BIM tools
│   │   ├── sandbox/        # Isolated execution environments
│   │   ├── services/       # Supabase, Redis, transcription
│   │   └── threads/        # Agent thread management
├── packages/
│   └── shared/         # @agentpress/shared (cross-platform types, utils)
├── sdk/                # Python SDK
└── infra/              # Pulumi infrastructure
```

---

### **2. BUILD, TEST, LINT COMMANDS**

#### **Root Commands** (`make <target>` from project root):

**Setup**:
- `make install` — Install all deps (pnpm + uv)
- `make install-frontend` — pnpm install (Next.js)
- `make install-backend` — uv sync (FastAPI)
- `make env-frontend` — Create `apps/frontend/.env.local`
- `make env-backend` — Create `backend/.env`

**Development (Local)**:
- `make dev` — Frontend + backend concurrently
- `make dev-frontend` — Next.js dev server only (:3000)
- `make dev-backend` — FastAPI dev server only (:8000)

**Docker (Full Stack)**:
- `make docker-up` — Start services (redis, backend, frontend)
- `make docker-down` — Stop all services
- `make docker-build` — Rebuild Docker images
- `make docker-logs`, `make docker-logs-api`, `make docker-logs-redis` — View logs
- `make restart` — Restart Docker services

**Build**:
- `make build` — Production build (Next.js)
- `make build-frontend` — Build frontend specifically

**Database**:
- `make db-migrate` — Apply Supabase migrations
- `make db-reset` — Reset local Supabase DB

**Testing**:
- `make test` — All tests (backend + E2E)
- `make test-backend` — Run pytest
- `make test-backend-cov` — Run pytest with coverage
- `make test-e2e` — Playwright E2E tests
- `make test-frontend` — Frontend tests (placeholder)

**Code Quality**:
- `make lint` — ESLint + Ruff
- `make lint-fix` — Auto-fix linting issues
- `make typecheck` — TypeScript type check
- `make format` — Prettier + Ruff format
- `make verify` — Backend build verification (check imports, syntax)
- `make check` — Full verification (verify + lint + format-check + typecheck)

#### **Backend Specific** (`cd backend && make/uv`):

- `make lint` — Ruff check on `core/`
- `make lint-fix` — Auto-fix with Ruff
- `make verify` — Build verification (run `core/utils/scripts/verify_build.py`)
- `make install-bim` — Install optional BIM packages (ifcopenshell, numpy, networkx, ladybug-core)
- `make test-bim` — Run BIM tool tests

**Backend Commands** (direct `uv run`):
- `uv run api.py` — Run FastAPI server
- `uv run uvicorn api:app --reload --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8000` — Dev server with hot reload
- `uv run pytest tests/ -v` — All tests
- `uv run pytest tests/ -k "test_name" -v` — Single test
- `uv run pytest tests/ -m e2e -v` — E2E tests only
- `uv run pytest tests/e2e/test_full_flow.py -v` — Full flow test

#### **Frontend Specific** (`cd apps/frontend && pnpm`):

- `pnpm dev` — Dev server with Turbopack (:3000)
- `pnpm build` — Production build
- `pnpm lint` — ESLint
- `pnpm format` — Prettier
- `pnpm test:e2e` — Playwright E2E tests
- `pnpm test:e2e:bim` — BIM-specific E2E tests

#### **Mobile Specific** (`cd apps/mobile && npm/yarn`):

- `npm run dev` — Start Expo dev server
- `npm run android` — Start Android emulator
- `npm run ios` — Start iOS simulator
- `npm run web` — Run on web

#### **Monorepo Commands** (from root with `pnpm --filter`):

- `pnpm dev:frontend` — Start frontend (filters to "Carbon BIM")
- `pnpm dev:mobile` — Start mobile (filters to "carbon-bim")
- `pnpm build:frontend` — Build frontend

---

### **3. BACKEND ARCHITECTURE**

#### **Main Entry Point**: `backend/api.py`

- Initializes FastAPI app with CORS middleware
- **Key imports**:
  ```python
  from core.agentpress.thread_manager import ThreadManager
  from core.services.supabase import DBConnection
  from core.services import redis
  ```
- **Routers registered**:
  - `versioning_router` — API versioning
  - `agent_runs_router` — Agent execution streams
  - `agent_crud_router` — Agent CRUD operations
  - `agent_tools_router` — Tool listing/configuration
  - `agent_setup_router` — Agent setup endpoints
  - `threads_router` — Thread management
  - `billing_router` — Credit/subscription handling
  - `setup_router` — Initial setup workflow
  - `sandbox_api` — Sandbox environment management
  - `admin_router` — Admin dashboard APIs
  - `auth_api` — Authentication

- **Key Features**:
  - Rate limiting (max 25 concurrent IPs)
  - Graceful shutdown handling
  - Memory watchdog & metrics logging
  - Instance ID tracking for distributed deployments
  - Health check endpoint

#### **Tool System**: `backend/core/agentpress/tool.py`

**Base Class: `Tool`** (abstract)
- Abstract base for all tool implementations
- Automatic schema & metadata registration

**Core Concepts**:

1. **ToolSchema** (dataclass):
   - Stores schema type (`SchemaType.OPENAPI`) and schema definition
   - Container for tool function signatures

2. **ToolResult** (dataclass):
   - `success: bool` — execution status
   - `output: Any` — result data (JSON string or plain text)

3. **ToolMetadata** (dataclass):
   ```python
   @dataclass
   class ToolMetadata:
       display_name: str
       description: str
       icon: Optional[str]
       color: Optional[str]
       is_core: bool
       weight: int           # Sort order (lower = higher priority)
       visible: bool         # Show in frontend UI
       usage_guide: Optional[str]
   ```

4. **MethodMetadata** (dataclass):
   ```python
   @dataclass
   class MethodMetadata:
       display_name: str
       description: str
       is_core: bool
       visible: bool
   ```

**Decorators**:

```python
# Tool-level metadata
@tool_metadata(
    display_name="Carbon Analysis",
    description="Calculate embodied carbon from IFC models",
    icon="Leaf",
    color="bg-green-100 dark:bg-green-800/50",
    weight=60,          # Priority (10=highest)
    visible=True,       # Show in UI
    usage_guide="Detailed doc loaded on-demand"
)
class CarbonCalculationTool(Tool):
    
    # Method-level schema (OpenAPI)
    @openapi_schema({
        "type": "function",
        "function": {
            "name": "calculate_carbon",
            "parameters": {...}
        }
    })
    async def calculate_carbon(self, file_path: str) -> ToolResult:
        # Implementation
        return self.success_response({"total_co2": 12500})

    # Helper methods
    def success_response(self, data: Dict|List|str) -> ToolResult
    def fail_response(self, msg: str) -> ToolResult
```

#### **Tool Registry**: `backend/core/tools/tool_registry.py`

**Tool Categories**:

1. **CORE_TOOLS** (always enabled):
   - `expand_msg_tool` — Expand messages
   - `message_tool` — Send messages
   - `task_list_tool` — Manage task lists
   - `sb_git_sync` — Git integration

2. **SANDBOX_TOOLS** (isolated execution):
   - `sb_shell_tool` — Execute shell commands
   - `sb_files_tool` — File operations
   - `sb_file_reader_tool` — Read file contents
   - `sb_vision_tool` — Image analysis
   - `sb_image_edit_tool` — Image manipulation
   - `sb_kb_tool` — Knowledge base
   - `sb_presentation_tool` — Create presentations
   - `sb_spreadsheet_tool` — Spreadsheet operations
   - `sb_canvas_tool` — Canvas drawing
   - `sb_upload_file_tool` — File uploads

3. **SEARCH_TOOLS**:
   - `web_search_tool` — Web search
   - `image_search_tool` — Image search
   - `people_search_tool` — People search
   - `company_search_tool` — Company search
   - `paper_search_tool` — Academic paper search

4. **BIM_TOOLS** (Building Information Modeling):
   - `bim_ifc_parser_tool` — Parse IFC files
   - `bim_carbon_tool` — Carbon footprint calculation
   - `bim_clash_tool` — Detect clashes between elements
   - `bim_compliance_tool` — Thai building code compliance
   - `bim_mep_tool` — MEP (Mechanical/Electrical/Plumbing) analysis
   - `bim_knowledge_graph_tool` — Build knowledge graph

5. **UTILITY_TOOLS**:
   - `browser_tool` — Browser automation
   - `vapi_voice_tool` — Voice interaction
   - `apify_tool` — Web scraping
   - `composio_upload_tool` — File upload

6. **AGENT_BUILDER_TOOLS**:
   - `agent_config_tool` — Configure agents
   - `mcp_search_tool` — Search MCP tools
   - `trigger_tool` — Setup agent triggers

**Registry Functions**:
```python
def get_all_tools() -> Dict[str, Type[Tool]]
def get_tool_class(module_path: str, class_name: str) -> Type[Tool]
def get_tool_info(tool_name: str) -> Optional[Tuple[str, str, str]]
def get_tools_by_category() -> Dict[str, List[Tuple[str, str, str]]]
def get_tool_usage_guide(tool_name: str) -> Optional[str]
```

#### **Agent Execution**: `backend/core/agentpress/thread_manager/`

**ThreadManager** (`manager.py` - 262 lines):
- Main coordinator for agent execution
- Manages message pipeline and LLM calls
- Handles streaming responses via Server-Sent Events (SSE)

**Message Pipeline**:
1. **MessageFetcher** — Retrieve messages from database

```tsx
import { Menu, Zap } from 'lucide-react-native';
import { Icon } from '@/components/ui/icon';

// ✅ GOOD
<Icon as={Menu} size={24} className="text-foreground" />

// ❌ BAD

```

**4. Animations — React Native Reanimated**:
```tsx
import Animated, { 
  useAnimatedStyle, 
  useSharedValue, 
  withSpring 
} from 'react-native-reanimated';

const scale = useSharedValue(1);
const animatedStyle = useAnimatedStyle(() => ({
  transform: [{ scale: scale.value }],
}));

scale.value = withSpring(0.9, { damping: 15, stiffness: 400 });
```

**5. Spacing — Tailwind Scale ONLY** (Never custom pixels):
```tsx
// ✅ GOOD
<View className="gap-3 p-4 mx-6 mb-8">

// ❌ BAD
<View style={{ gap: 11.25, padding: 15.5 }}>

// Spacing scale: gap-1 to gap-12 (4px to 48px)
```

**6. Logic Extraction — Custom Hooks**:
```tsx
// ✅ GOOD — Logic in hooks
function useAgentManager() {
  const [agent, setAgent] = useState(DEFAULT_AGENT);
  return { agent, setAgent };
}

export function HomeScreen() {
  const { agent } = useAgentManager();
  return <View>{/* ... */}</View>;
}

// ❌ BAD — Logic mixed with component
export function HomeScreen() {
  const [agent, setAgent] = useState(DEFAULT_AGENT);
  // 50 more lines of logic
  return <View>{/* ... */}</View>;
}
```

**7. Console Logging with Emojis**:
```tsx
console.log('🎯 Action:', 'Menu pressed');
console.log('⏰ Timestamp:', new Date().toISOString());
console.log('📊 Data:', { userId, screen: 'Home' });
console.log('✅ Success');
console.log('❌ Error');
console.log('🌓 Theme changed');
```

**8. Component Organization**:
- One component per file
- Create `index.ts` for clean imports
- Co-locate types in `types.ts`
- Extract utilities to separate files

**9. TypeScript Strict Mode**:
- No implicit `any`
- Always explicit types
- Avoid type unions when possible

**10. Theme Support**:
```tsx
import { useColorScheme } from 'nativewind';

const { colorScheme } = useColorScheme();

if (colorScheme === 'dark') {
  // Dark mode logic
}
```

- `expo-router` — File-based routing

**Navigation**:
- `@react-navigation/native`
- `react-native-screens`
- `react-native-gesture-handler`

**Media**:
- `expo-image-picker` — Photos
- `expo-audio` — Audio recording
- `expo-av` — Video playback
- `lottie-react-native` — Animations

#### **Shared Package** (`@agentpress/shared`)

**Exports**:
- `./types` — Message, Tool, streaming types
- `./streaming` — `useAgentStreamCore`, message handlers
- `./tools` — Tool parser, formatter
- `./utils` — JSON parser, normalizers
- `./animations` — `useSmoothText`, `useSmoothAnimation`
- `./constants` — Upload limits
- `./errors` — Error types

**Dependency**: `zustand@5.0.9`

---

### **8. API ENDPOINTS & HEALTH CHECKS**

**Base URL**:
- Dev: `http://localhost:8000/v1`
- Prod: Configured via `NEXT_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL`

**Key Endpoints**:
- `GET /health` — Health check
- `POST /agent/start` — Start agent run
- `GET /agent-run/{id}/stream` — Stream agent output (SSE)
- `POST /agent-run/{id}/stop` — Stop agent
- `GET /tools` — List all tools
- `GET /tools/{category}` — Tools by category
- `POST /agents` — Create agent
- `GET /agents/{id}` — Get agent details
- `POST /threads` — Create thread

---

### **9. ENVIRONMENT CONFIGURATION**

#### **Backend** (`backend/.env`):
```
SUPABASE_URL=...
SUPABASE_KEY=...
REDIS_HOST=localhost
REDIS_PORT=6379
ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=...          # Claude
OPENAI_API_KEY=...             # GPT
BIM_CARBON_DATABASE_PATH=/data/carbon_factors.json
BIM_THAI_CODES_PATH=/data/thai_building_codes.json
BIM_MAX_FILE_SIZE_MB=500
```

#### **Frontend** (`apps/frontend/.env.local`):
```
NEXT_PUBLIC_ENV_MODE="staging"
NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL="..."
NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY="..."
NEXT_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL="http://localhost:8000/v1"
NEXT_PUBLIC_URL="http://localhost:3000"
```

---

### **10. DEPLOYMENT & CI/CD**

**Branch Strategy**:
- `main` → DEV (auto-deploy)
- `staging` → STAGING (auto-deploy)
- `production` → Production (requires approval)

**Docker Compose**:
- `docker compose up -d` — Start all (Redis + Backend + Frontend)
- Backend: http://localhost:8000
- Frontend: http://localhost:3000
- Redis: localhost:6379

**Dokploy** (Production Deployment):
- `make dokploy-up` — Deploy
- `make dokploy-cli-deploy APP_ID=<id>` — Remote deploy via CLI

**Infrastructure**:
- AWS instances with Cloudflare tunnels
- Pulumi stacks for environment management
- GitHub Actions for CI/CD

---

### **11. QUICK REFERENCE: MOST IMPORTANT PATTERNS**

**Backend Tool Pattern**:
```python
@tool_metadata(display_name="...", description="...", icon="...", weight=60, visible=True)
class MyTool(Tool):
    @openapi_schema({...})
    async def my_method(self, param: str) -> ToolResult:
        return self.success_response({"result": value})
```

**Frontend Component Pattern**:
```tsx
import { Button } from '@/components/ui/button';

export function MyComponent() {
  return <button>Click me</button>;
}
```

**Mobile Component Pattern**:
```tsx
// ✅ Design tokens ONLY, Roobert ONLY, logic in hooks, Lucide ONLY
<View className="bg-background p-4">
  <Text className="text-foreground font-roobert-medium">Title</Text>
  <Icon as={Menu} size={24} className="text-foreground" />
</View>
```

**Carbon Calculation**:
```typescript
// Frontend: apps/frontend/src/lib/carbon/calculator.ts
const carbon = calculateItemCarbon({
  quantity: 100,
  unit: 'm3',
  materialId: 'concrete',
  transportDistance: 50,
  transportVehicle: 'truck_10wheel'
});
```

---

# Claude Code Behavioral Rules

Actionable rules for enhanced Claude Code framework operation.

## Rule Priority System

**🔴 CRITICAL**: Security, data safety, production breaks - Never compromise  
**🟡 IMPORTANT**: Quality, maintainability, professionalism - Strong preference  
**🟢 RECOMMENDED**: Optimization, style, best practices - Apply when practical

### Conflict Resolution Hierarchy
1. **Safety First**: Security/data rules always win
2. **Scope > Features**: Build only what's asked > complete everything  
3. **Quality > Speed**: Except in genuine emergencies
4. **Context Matters**: Prototype vs Production requirements differ

## Agent Orchestration
**Priority**: 🔴 **Triggers**: Task execution and post-implementation

**Task Execution Layer** (Existing Auto-Activation):
- **Auto-Selection**: Claude Code automatically selects appropriate specialist agents based on context
- **Keywords**: Security, performance, frontend, backend, architecture keywords trigger specialist agents
- **File Types**: `.py`, `.jsx`, `.ts`, etc. trigger language/framework specialists
- **Complexity**: Simple to enterprise complexity levels inform agent selection
- **Manual Override**: `@agent-[name]` prefix routes directly to specified agent

**Self-Improvement Layer** (PM Agent Meta-Layer):
- **Post-Implementation**: PM Agent activates after task completion to document learnings
- **Mistake Detection**: PM Agent activates immediately when errors occur for root cause analysis
- **Monthly Maintenance**: PM Agent performs systematic documentation health reviews
- **Knowledge Capture**: Transforms experiences into reusable patterns and best practices
- **Documentation Evolution**: Maintains fresh, minimal, high-signal documentation

**Orchestration Flow**:
1. **Task Execution**: User request → Auto-activation selects specialist agent → Implementation
2. **Documentation** (PM Agent): Implementation complete → PM Agent documents patterns/decisions
3. **Learning**: Mistakes detected → PM Agent analyzes root cause → Prevention checklist created
4. **Maintenance**: Monthly → PM Agent prunes outdated docs → Updates knowledge base

✅ **Right**: User request → backend-architect implements → PM Agent documents patterns
✅ **Right**: Error detected → PM Agent stops work → Root cause analysis → Documentation updated
✅ **Right**: `@agent-security "review auth"` → Direct to security-engineer (manual override)
❌ **Wrong**: Skip documentation after implementation (no PM Agent activation)
❌ **Wrong**: Continue implementing after mistake (no root cause analysis)

## Workflow Rules
**Priority**: 🟡 **Triggers**: All development tasks

- **Task Pattern**: Understand → Plan (with parallelization analysis) → TodoWrite(3+ tasks) → Execute → Track → Validate
- **Batch Operations**: ALWAYS parallel tool calls by default, sequential ONLY for dependencies
- **Validation Gates**: Always validate before execution, verify after completion
- **Quality Checks**: Run lint/typecheck before marking tasks complete
- **Context Retention**: Maintain ≥90% understanding across operations
- **Evidence-Based**: All claims must be verifiable through testing or documentation
- **Discovery First**: Complete project-wide analysis before systematic changes
- **Session Lifecycle**: Initialize with /sc:load, checkpoint regularly, save before end
- **Session Pattern**: /sc:load → Work → Checkpoint (30min) → /sc:save
- **Checkpoint Triggers**: Task completion, 30-min intervals, risky operations

✅ **Right**: Plan → TodoWrite → Execute → Validate
❌ **Wrong**: Jump directly to implementation without planning

## Planning Efficiency
**Priority**: 🔴 **Triggers**: All planning phases, TodoWrite operations, multi-step tasks

- **Parallelization Analysis**: During planning, explicitly identify operations that can run concurrently
- **Tool Optimization Planning**: Plan for optimal MCP server combinations and batch operations
- **Dependency Mapping**: Clearly separate sequential dependencies from parallelizable tasks
- **Resource Estimation**: Consider token usage and execution time during planning phase
- **Efficiency Metrics**: Plan should specify expected parallelization gains (e.g., "3 parallel ops = 60% time saving")

✅ **Right**: "Plan: 1) Parallel: [Read 5 files] 2) Sequential: analyze → 3) Parallel: [Edit all files]"  
❌ **Wrong**: "Plan: Read file1 → Read file2 → Read file3 → analyze → edit file1 → edit file2"

## Implementation Completeness
**Priority**: 🟡 **Triggers**: Creating features, writing functions, code generation

- **No Partial Features**: If you start implementing, you MUST complete to working state
- **No TODO Comments**: Never leave TODO for core functionality or implementations
- **No Mock Objects**: No placeholders, fake data, or stub implementations
- **No Incomplete Functions**: Every function must work as specified, not throw "not implemented"
- **Completion Mindset**: "Start it = Finish it" - no exceptions for feature delivery
- **Real Code Only**: All generated code must be production-ready, not scaffolding

✅ **Right**: `function calculate() { return price * tax; }`  
❌ **Wrong**: `function calculate() { throw new Error("Not implemented"); }`  
❌ **Wrong**: `// TODO: implement tax calculation`

## Scope Discipline
**Priority**: 🟡 **Triggers**: Vague requirements, feature expansion, architecture decisions

- **Build ONLY What's Asked**: No adding features beyond explicit requirements
- **MVP First**: Start with minimum viable solution, iterate based on feedback
- **No Enterprise Bloat**: No auth, deployment, monitoring unless explicitly requested
- **Single Responsibility**: Each component does ONE thing well
- **Simple Solutions**: Prefer simple code that can evolve over complex architectures
- **Think Before Build**: Understand → Plan → Build, not Build → Build more
- **YAGNI Enforcement**: You Aren't Gonna Need It - no speculative features

✅ **Right**: "Build login form" → Just login form  
===
# Software Engineering Principles

**Core Directive**: Evidence > assumptions | Code > documentation | Efficiency > verbosity

## Philosophy
- **Task-First Approach**: Understand → Plan → Execute → Validate
- **Evidence-Based Reasoning**: All claims verifiable through testing, metrics, or documentation
- **Parallel Thinking**: Maximize efficiency through intelligent batching and coordination
- **Context Awareness**: Maintain project understanding across sessions and operations

## Engineering Mindset

### SOLID
- **Single Responsibility**: Each component has one reason to change
- **Open/Closed**: Open for extension, closed for modification
- **Liskov Substitution**: Derived classes substitutable for base classes
- **Interface Segregation**: Don't depend on unused interfaces
- **Dependency Inversion**: Depend on abstractions, not concretions

### Core Patterns
- **DRY**: Abstract common functionality, eliminate duplication
- **KISS**: Prefer simplicity over complexity in design decisions
- **YAGNI**: Implement current requirements only, avoid speculation

### Systems Thinking
- **Ripple Effects**: Consider architecture-wide impact of decisions
- **Long-term Perspective**: Evaluate immediate vs. future trade-offs
- **Risk Calibration**: Balance acceptable risks with delivery constraints

## Decision Framework

### Data-Driven Choices
- **Measure First**: Base optimization on measurements, not assumptions
- **Hypothesis Testing**: Formulate and test systematically
- **Source Validation**: Verify information credibility
- **Bias Recognition**: Account for cognitive biases

### Trade-off Analysis
- **Temporal Impact**: Immediate vs. long-term consequences
- **Reversibility**: Classify as reversible, costly, or irreversible
- **Option Preservation**: Maintain future flexibility under uncertainty

### Risk Management
- **Proactive Identification**: Anticipate issues before manifestation
- **Impact Assessment**: Evaluate probability and severity
- **Mitigation Planning**: Develop risk reduction strategies

## Quality Philosophy

### Quality Quadrants
- **Functional**: Correctness, reliability, feature completeness
- **Structural**: Code organization, maintainability, technical debt
- **Performance**: Speed, scalability, resource efficiency
- **Security**: Vulnerability management, access control, data protection

### Quality Standards
- **Automated Enforcement**: Use tooling for consistent quality
- **Preventive Measures**: Catch issues early when cheaper to fix
- **Human-Centered Design**: Prioritize user welfare and autonomy
<exited with exit code 0>

### 💬 Copilot

Created `.github/copilot-instructions.md`. Here's a summary of what's in it:

**Commands** — All `make`, `uv run`, `pnpm`, and `expo` commands for backend, frontend, and mobile, including single-test invocations and the optional BIM package install.

**Architecture** — The monorepo layout, agent execution flow (SSE streaming via ThreadManager), BIM workflow (IFC → analysis → 3D viewer), tool registry categories, and the shared `@agentpress/shared` package.

**Key conventions**:
- Backend Tool pattern (the `@tool_metadata` + `@openapi_schema` decorator pair, `success_response`/`fail_response`)
- How to register a new tool in `tool_registry.py`
- Python import grouping and error handling patterns
- Pytest markers
- Mobile strict rules (Roobert font, design tokens only, Lucide icons, Reanimated spring config, Tailwind spacing only, logic-in-hooks)
- Never-commit-without-request rule

# ============================================================================
# CARBON-BIM PRODUCTION DOCKER COMPOSE
# ============================================================================
# Production deployment with Traefik reverse proxy for HTTPS
#
# Usage:
#   docker compose -f docker-compose.prod.yaml up -d
#
# Custom Domain: carbon-bim.ensimu.space (via Cloudflare DNS)
# ============================================================================

services:
  traefik:
    image: traefik:v3.0
    container_name: traefik
    restart: unless-stopped
    command:
      - "--api.dashboard=false"
      - "--providers.docker=true"
      - "--providers.docker.exposedbydefault=false"
      - "--entrypoints.web.address=:80"
      - "--entrypoints.websecure.address=:443"
      # Redirect HTTP to HTTPS
      - "--entrypoints.web.http.redirections.entryPoint.to=websecure"
      - "--entrypoints.web.http.redirections.entryPoint.scheme=https"
      # Use Cloudflare for SSL (Flexible mode - Cloudflare handles SSL)
    ports:
      - "80:80"
      - "443:443"
    volumes:
      - /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock:ro
    networks:
      - web

  redis:
    image: redis:7-alpine
    container_name: carbon-bim-redis
    restart: unless-stopped
    volumes:
      - redis_data:/data
      - ./backend/services/docker/redis.conf:/usr/local/etc/redis/redis.conf:ro
    command: redis-server /usr/local/etc/redis/redis.conf --save 60 1 --loglevel warning
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "redis-cli", "ping"]
      interval: 10s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 3
    networks:
      - internal

  backend:
    image: ghcr.io/suna-ai/suna-backend:latest
    container_name: carbon-bim-backend
    platform: linux/amd64
    restart: unless-stopped
    build:
      context: ./backend
      dockerfile: Dockerfile
    volumes:
      - ./backend/.env:/app/.env:ro
    environment:
      - REDIS_HOST=redis
      - REDIS_PORT=6379
      - REDIS_PASSWORD=
      - REDIS_SSL=False
    dns:
      - 8.8.8.8
      - 1.1.1.1
    depends_on:
      redis:
        condition: service_healthy
    labels:
      - "traefik.enable=true"
      - "traefik.http.routers.backend.rule=Host(`carbon-bim.ensimu.space`) && PathPrefix(`/api`, `/v1`)"
      - "traefik.http.routers.backend.entrypoints=websecure"
      - "traefik.http.routers.backend.tls=true"
      - "traefik.http.services.backend.loadbalancer.server.port=8000"
      # Strip /api prefix if needed
      - "traefik.http.middlewares.strip-api.stripprefix.prefixes=/api"
      - "traefik.http.routers.backend.middlewares=strip-api@docker"
    networks:
      - web
      - internal

  frontend:
    container_name: carbon-bim-frontend
    restart: unless-stopped
    init: true
    build:
      context: .
      dockerfile: apps/frontend/Dockerfile
      args:
        NEXT_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL: ${NEXT_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL:-https://carbon-bim.ensimu.space/api}
        NEXT_PUBLIC_URL: ${NEXT_PUBLIC_URL:-https://carbon-bim.ensimu.space}
        NEXT_PUBLIC_ENV_MODE: ${NEXT_PUBLIC_ENV_MODE:-production}
        NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL: ${NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL:-}
        NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY: ${NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY:-}
    depends_on:
      - backend
    labels:
      - "traefik.enable=true"
      - "traefik.http.routers.frontend.rule=Host(`carbon-bim.ensimu.space`)"
      - "traefik.http.routers.frontend.entrypoints=websecure"
      - "traefik.http.routers.frontend.tls=true"
      - "traefik.http.services.frontend.loadbalancer.server.port=3000"
      # Frontend has lower priority than backend API routes
      - "traefik.http.routers.frontend.priority=1"
    networks:
      - web

volumes:
  redis_data:

networks:
  web:
    external: false
  internal:
    external: false
===
# =============================================================================
# SUNA BIM AGENT — DOKPLOY PRODUCTION COMPOSE
# =============================================================================
# Dokploy reads this file to build and deploy all services.
# All secrets use ${VAR} syntax — set them in the Dokploy dashboard under
# "Environment Variables" for each service (or project-level).
#
# Usage:
#   make dokploy-build   # Build images
#   make dokploy-up      # Start services (detached)
#   make dokploy-down    # Stop services
#   make dokploy-logs    # Tail logs
# =============================================================================

networks:
  suna_internal:
    driver: bridge

volumes:
  redis_data:
    driver: local

services:
  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  # Redis — internal cache/queue, NOT exposed externally
  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  redis:
    image: redis:7-alpine
    restart: unless-stopped
    mem_limit: 768m
    command: redis-server --save 60 1 --loglevel warning
    volumes:
      - redis_data:/data
    networks:
      - suna_internal
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "redis-cli", "ping"]
      interval: 10s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 5
      start_period: 10s

  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  # Backend — Python FastAPI (port 40 → 8000)
  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  backend:
    build:
      context: ./backend
      dockerfile: Dockerfile
    restart: unless-stopped
    mem_limit: 768m
    ports:
      - "40:8000"
    networks:
      - suna_internal
    dns:
      - 8.8.8.8
      - 1.1.1.1
    environment:
      # ── Supabase ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
      SUPABASE_URL: ${SUPABASE_URL}
      SUPABASE_ANON_KEY: ${SUPABASE_ANON_KEY}
      SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY: ${SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY}
      SUPABASE_JWT_SECRET: ${SUPABASE_JWT_SECRET}
      # ── LLM Providers ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
      ANTHROPIC_API_KEY: ${ANTHROPIC_API_KEY:-}
      OPENAI_API_KEY: ${OPENAI_API_KEY:-}
      # ── Sandbox ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
      E2B_API_KEY: ${E2B_API_KEY}
      # ── Search / Crawl ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
      TAVILY_API_KEY: ${TAVILY_API_KEY}
      FIRECRAWL_API_KEY: ${FIRECRAWL_API_KEY}
      FIRECRAWL_URL: ${FIRECRAWL_URL:-https://api.firecrawl.dev}
      # ── Redis (internal service name) ─────────────────────────────────────
      REDIS_HOST: redis
      REDIS_PORT: 6379
      REDIS_PASSWORD: ${REDIS_PASSWORD:-}
      REDIS_SSL: ${REDIS_SSL:-False}
      # ── BIM-specific ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
      BIM_CARBON_DATABASE_PATH: ${BIM_CARBON_DATABASE_PATH:-/data/carbon_factors.json}
      BIM_THAI_CODES_PATH: ${BIM_THAI_CODES_PATH:-/data/thai_building_codes.json}
      BIM_MAX_FILE_SIZE_MB: ${BIM_MAX_FILE_SIZE_MB:-500}
      # ── Runtime ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
      ENV_MODE: ${ENV_MODE:-production}
      WORKERS: ${WORKERS:-1}
      ENCRYPTION_KEY: ${ENCRYPTION_KEY}
      DATABASE_URL: ${DATABASE_URL}
      MCP_CREDENTIAL_ENCRYPTION_KEY: ${MCP_CREDENTIAL_ENCRYPTION_KEY}
    depends_on:
      redis:
        condition: service_started
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "curl", "-f", "http://localhost:8000/v1/health"]
      interval: 30s
      timeout: 10s
      retries: 3
      start_period: 30s

  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  # Frontend — Next.js (port 30 → 3000)
  # Build args bake NEXT_PUBLIC_* values into the static bundle at build time.
  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  frontend:
    init: true
    build:
      context: .
      dockerfile: apps/frontend/Dockerfile
      args:
        NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL: ${NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL}
        NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY: ${NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY}
        NEXT_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL: ${NEXT_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL}
        NEXT_PUBLIC_URL: ${NEXT_PUBLIC_URL}
        NEXT_PUBLIC_ENV_MODE: ${NEXT_PUBLIC_ENV_MODE:-production}
        PNPM_LOCKFILE_MODE: ${PNPM_LOCKFILE_MODE:---frozen-lockfile}
    restart: unless-stopped
    mem_limit: 768m
    ports:
      - "30:3000"
    networks:
      - suna_internal
    depends_on:
      backend:
        condition: service_started
<exited with exit code 0>
```

</details>

### ✅ `bash`

**Read CI/CD workflow**

$ cat /teamspace/studios/this_studio/suna-bim-agent/.github/workflows/deploy-azure-vm.yml 2>/dev/null

<details>
<summary>102 lines</summary>

```
name: Deploy to Azure VM

on:
  push:
    branches:
      - main
      - staging
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      environment:
        description: 'Environment to deploy to'
        required: true
        default: 'production'
        type: choice
        options:
          - production
          - staging

concurrency:
  group: deploy-azure-${{ github.ref_name }}
  cancel-in-progress: false

jobs:
  deploy:
    name: Deploy to Azure VM
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Deploy via SSH
        uses: appleboy/ssh-action@v1
        env:
          BRANCH: ${{ github.ref_name }}
          DATABASE_URL: ${{ secrets.DATABASE_URL }}
          SUPABASE_URL: ${{ secrets.SUPABASE_URL }}
          SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY: ${{ secrets.SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY }}
          SUPABASE_ANON_KEY: ${{ secrets.SUPABASE_ANON_KEY }}
          ENCRYPTION_KEY: ${{ secrets.ENCRYPTION_KEY }}
        with:
          host: ${{ secrets.AZURE_VM_HOST }}
          username: ${{ secrets.AZURE_VM_USERNAME }}
          key: ${{ secrets.AZURE_VM_SSH_KEY }}
          port: 22
          timeout: 300s
          debug: false
          envs: BRANCH,DATABASE_URL,SUPABASE_URL,SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY,SUPABASE_ANON_KEY,ENCRYPTION_KEY
          script: |
            set -e
            echo "🚀 Deploying to Azure VM..."

            cd ~/suna-bim-agent

            echo "📥 Pulling latest code..."
            git fetch origin "$BRANCH"
            git reset --hard "origin/$BRANCH"

            echo "🔐 Updating environment variables..."
            cd backend
            echo "ENCRYPTION_KEY=${ENCRYPTION_KEY}" > .env
            echo "NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL=${SUPABASE_URL}" >> .env
            echo "NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY=${SUPABASE_ANON_KEY}" >> .env
            echo "SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY=${SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY}" >> .env
            echo "SUPABASE_ANON_KEY=${SUPABASE_ANON_KEY}" >> .env
            echo "SUPABASE_URL=${SUPABASE_URL}" >> .env
            echo "DATABASE_URL=${DATABASE_URL}" >> .env
            cd ..

            echo "📦 Installing dependencies..."
            pnpm install --frozen-lockfile

            echo "🔨 Building frontend..."
            cd apps/frontend
            export NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL="${SUPABASE_URL}"
            export NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY="${SUPABASE_ANON_KEY}"
            export NEXT_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL="https://carbon-bim.ensimu.space/api"
            export NEXT_PUBLIC_URL="https://carbon-bim.ensimu.space"
            export NEXT_PUBLIC_ENV_MODE="production"
            pnpm build
            cd ../..

            echo "🔄 Restarting services..."
            # Export env vars for docker-compose build
            export NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL="${SUPABASE_URL}"
            export NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY="${SUPABASE_ANON_KEY}"
            export NEXT_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL="https://carbon-bim.ensimu.space/api"
            export NEXT_PUBLIC_URL="https://carbon-bim.ensimu.space"
            export NEXT_PUBLIC_ENV_MODE="production"

            # Use production compose with Traefik
            if [ -f "docker-compose.prod.yaml" ]; then
              docker compose -f docker-compose.prod.yaml down || true
              docker compose -f docker-compose.prod.yaml up -d --build
              docker image prune -af --filter "until=24h" || true
            elif [ -f "docker-compose.yaml" ] || [ -f "docker-compose.yml" ]; then
              docker compose down || true
              docker compose up -d --build
              docker image prune -af --filter "until=24h" || true
            fi

            echo "✅ Deployment complete!"

      - name: Verify deployment
        uses: appleboy/ssh-action@v1
        with:
          host: ${{ secrets.AZURE_VM_HOST }}
          username: ${{ secrets.AZURE_VM_USERNAME }}
          key: ${{ secrets.AZURE_VM_SSH_KEY }}
          port: 22
          timeout: 60s
          script: |
            echo "🔍 Verifying deployment..."
            cd ~/suna-bim-agent
            docker compose -f docker-compose.prod.yaml ps || docker compose ps || true
            curl -sf http://localhost:3000 > /dev/null && echo "✅ Frontend OK" || echo "⚠️ Frontend check failed"
            curl -sf http://localhost:8000/v1/health > /dev/null && echo "✅ Backend OK" || echo "⚠️ Backend check failed"
<exited with exit code 0>
```

</details>

````
# Deploying Suna BIM Agent with Dokploy

Dokploy is a self-hosted PaaS built on Docker Compose + Traefik. This guide walks you through deploying the full Suna BIM Agent stack (Frontend, Backend, Redis) to a Dokploy server.

---

## Prerequisites

| Requirement | Notes |
|---|---|
| Dokploy server | VPS with ≥ 2 CPU, 4 GB RAM (8 GB recommended for IFC processing) |
| Docker ≥ 24 | Installed by the Dokploy installer |
| Domain name | DNS A record pointing to your server IP |
| Supabase project | Cloud-hosted; get credentials from the Supabase dashboard |
| API keys | Anthropic and/or OpenAI (see `.env.dokploy`) |

---

## 1. Install Dokploy

SSH into your server and run the official installer:

```bash
curl -sSL https://dokploy.com/install.sh | sh
```

Open `http://<server-ip>:3000` and complete the initial setup wizard (admin account, domain).

---

## 2. Connect Your GitHub Repository

1. In the Dokploy dashboard, go to **Projects → New Project**.
2. Choose **Docker Compose** as the deployment type.
3. Connect your GitHub account and select the `autonomous-bim-agent` repository.
4. Set the **Compose File Path** to:
   ```
   suna/docker-compose.dokploy.yml
   ```
5. Set the **Build Context** (working directory) to:
   ```
   suna/
   ```

---

## 3. Configure Environment Variables

In the Dokploy project settings, open **Environment Variables** and paste in each value from `.env.dokploy`.

### Getting the Supabase JWT Secret

1. Open [Supabase Dashboard](https://supabase.com/dashboard) → your project.
2. Go to **Project Settings → API**.
3. Scroll to **JWT Settings** — copy the **JWT Secret** field.
4. Paste it as `SUPABASE_JWT_SECRET` in Dokploy.

### Minimum required variables before first deploy

| Variable | Where to get it |
|---|---|
| `SUPABASE_JWT_SECRET` | Supabase Dashboard → Project Settings → API → JWT Settings |
| `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` | [console.anthropic.com/settings/keys](https://console.anthropic.com/settings/keys) |
| `OPENAI_API_KEY` | [platform.openai.com/api-keys](https://platform.openai.com/api-keys) |
| `NEXT_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL` | `https://<your-domain>/api/v1` |
| `NEXT_PUBLIC_URL` | `https://<your-domain>` |

All other variables have safe defaults pre-filled in `.env.dokploy`.

---

## 4. Set Up Reverse Proxy (Traefik Labels)

Dokploy runs Traefik automatically. To route your domain to the correct services, add Traefik labels to each service in `docker-compose.dokploy.yml`, or configure them in the Dokploy **Domains** tab:

| Service | Path prefix | Target port |
|---|---|---|
| `frontend` | `/` (catch-all) | 3000 |
| `backend` | `/api/v1` | 8000 |

In the Dokploy dashboard → your project → **Domains**:
- Add `suna-bim.yourdomain.com` → service `frontend`, port `3000`
- Add `suna-bim.yourdomain.com/api` → service `backend`, port `8000` (path strip `/api`)

Dokploy will automatically provision a Let's Encrypt TLS certificate.

---

## 5. Deploy

Click **Deploy** in the Dokploy dashboard, or push to your configured branch (e.g. `main`). Dokploy will:

1. Pull the latest code from GitHub.
2. Build Docker images (`docker compose build`).
3. Start services in dependency order: `redis` → `backend` → `frontend`.
4. Run healthchecks — the backend must pass `GET /health` before the frontend starts.

Monitor progress in **Deployments → Logs**.

---

## 6. Verify the Deployment

```bash
# Backend health
curl https://suna-bim.yourdomain.com/api/v1/health

# Frontend
curl -I https://suna-bim.yourdomain.com
```

---

## Makefile Quick Reference

These targets work for both local validation and CI:

```bash
make dokploy-setup   # Copy .env.dokploy → .env for local testing
make dokploy-build   # Build production images locally
make dokploy-up      # Start the production stack locally
make dokploy-down    # Stop the production stack
make dokploy-logs    # Tail all service logs
```

---

## Deploy via Dokploy CLI (Alternative)

If you prefer automating deployment from your terminal instead of using the dashboard, use the [`@dokploy/cli`](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@dokploy/cli).

### 1. Install the CLI

```bash
make dokploy-cli-install
# or: npm install -g @dokploy/cli
```

### 2. Set your server URL and token

Edit `.env.dokploy` and fill in `DOKPLOY_SERVER_URL`:

```bash
DOKPLOY_SERVER_URL=https://your-panel-domain.com   # ← your Dokploy panel URL
DOKPLOY_TOKEN=aDYMjp...  # already set
```

Then load them into your shell:

```bash
===
# CI/CD Setup Guide

## Branch Strategy

```
main (DEV) ──promote──> staging (PRE-PROD) ──promote──> PRODUCTION
```

| Branch | Environment | Endpoint | Auto-Deploy |
|--------|-------------|----------|-------------|
| `main` | DEV | dev-api.kortix.com | ✅ On push |
| `staging` | STAGING | staging-api.kortix.com | ✅ On push |
| `PRODUCTION` | PRODUCTION | api.kortix.com | ✅ On push |

## Workflow

1. **Development**: Work on feature branches, merge PRs to `main`
2. **Testing**: `main` auto-deploys to DEV for testing
3. **Pre-prod**: Promote `main` → `staging` when ready for QA
4. **Production**: Promote `staging` → `PRODUCTION` when approved

## GitHub Secrets (Repository Level)

All secrets are configured at the repository level:

### DEV Environment
- `AWS_DEV_HOST`: `52.24.67.100`
- `AWS_DEV_USERNAME`: `ubuntu`
- `AWS_DEV_KEY`: SSH private key for dev instance

### STAGING Environment
- `AWS_STAGING_HOST`: `54.184.54.33`
- `AWS_STAGING_USERNAME`: `ubuntu`
- `AWS_STAGING_KEY`: SSH private key for staging instance

### PRODUCTION Environment
- `AWS_PRODUCTION_HOST`: `54.148.221.72`
- `AWS_PRODUCTION_USERNAME`: `ubuntu`
- `AWS_PRODUCTION_KEY`: SSH private key for prod Lightsail
- `AWS_DEPLOYMENT_ROLE`: IAM role ARN for ECS deployment

## GitHub Environments

Three environments are configured for deployment protection:
- `dev` - No protection (auto-deploy)
- `staging` - No protection (auto-deploy)
- `production` - Add required reviewers for extra safety

## Branch Protection Rules

### `main` branch
- ✅ Require pull request before merging
- ✅ Require approvals: 1
- ✅ Require status checks to pass (optional)
- ❌ Do not require linear history (allow merges)

### `staging` branch
- ✅ Require pull request before merging
- ✅ Require approvals: 1
- ✅ Restrict who can push: Only via promote workflow

### `PRODUCTION` branch
- ✅ Require pull request before merging
- ✅ Require approvals: 2
- ✅ Require review from code owners
- ✅ Restrict who can push: Only via promote workflow
- ✅ Lock branch (only allow promotions)

## Promoting Changes

### Option 1: Manual (GitHub UI)
1. Go to Actions → "Promote Branch"
2. Click "Run workflow"
3. Select promotion path:
   - `main → staging` (for pre-prod)
   - `staging → PRODUCTION` (for production)
4. Type "promote" to confirm
5. Click "Run workflow"

### Option 2: Scheduled (Automatic)
- Every Monday at 23:59 UTC: `staging → PRODUCTION`
- Disable in workflow if not needed

## Infrastructure Mapping

| Environment | Instance | IP | Tunnel ID |
|-------------|----------|-----|-----------|
| DEV | suna-dev | 52.24.67.100 | 3a533a53-67d0-487c-b716-261c863270ee |
| STAGING | suna-staging | 54.184.54.33 | 503813f5-2426-401a-b72f-15bd11d4b4ba |
| PRODUCTION | suna-prod | 54.148.221.72 | f4125d84-33d5-424d-ae6b-2b84b790392b |
| PRODUCTION (ECS) | suna-ecs | ALB | N/A |

## Pulumi Stacks

```bash
# View stack outputs
cd infra/environments/dev && pulumi stack output
cd infra/environments/staging && pulumi stack output
cd infra/environments/prod && pulumi stack output
```

# Quick start (Docker):  make docker-up
# =============================================================================

.DEFAULT_GOAL := help
.PHONY: help \
        install install-frontend install-backend \
--
        docker-up docker-down docker-build docker-logs docker-logs-api docker-logs-redis docker-ps \
        dokploy-setup dokploy-build dokploy-up dokploy-down dokploy-logs dokploy-validate \
        dokploy-cli-install dokploy-cli-auth dokploy-cli-verify dokploy-cli-projects dokploy-cli-deploy dokploy-cli-env-push \
        test test-e2e test-frontend test-backend test-backend-cov \
        lint lint-frontend lint-backend lint-fix \
        format format-check typecheck \
        verify check \
        db-migrate db-reset \
--
	@printf "  $(GREEN)docker-up$(RESET)          Start all services (detached)\n"
	@printf "  $(GREEN)docker-down$(RESET)        Stop all services\n"
	@printf "  $(GREEN)docker-build$(RESET)       Rebuild Docker images\n"
	@printf "  $(GREEN)docker-logs$(RESET)        Tail all logs\n"
	@printf "  $(GREEN)docker-logs-api$(RESET)    Tail API logs only\n"
	@printf "  $(GREEN)docker-logs-redis$(RESET)  Tail Redis logs only\n"
	@printf "  $(GREEN)docker-ps$(RESET)          Show running containers\n"
	@printf "  $(GREEN)restart$(RESET)            docker-down + docker-up\n"
	@printf "\n$(BOLD)━━ Dokploy (production) ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━$(RESET)\n"
	@printf "  $(GREEN)dokploy-validate$(RESET)      Validate compose file syntax\n"
	@printf "  $(GREEN)dokploy-setup$(RESET)         Copy .env.dokploy → .env for local test\n"
	@printf "  $(GREEN)dokploy-build$(RESET)         Build production images\n"
	@printf "  $(GREEN)dokploy-up$(RESET)            Start production stack (detached)\n"
	@printf "  $(GREEN)dokploy-down$(RESET)          Stop production stack\n"
	@printf "  $(GREEN)dokploy-logs$(RESET)          Tail production logs\n"
	@printf "\n$(BOLD)━━ Dokploy CLI ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━$(RESET)\n"
	@printf "  $(GREEN)dokploy-cli-install$(RESET)   Install @dokploy/cli globally\n"
	@printf "  $(GREEN)dokploy-cli-auth$(RESET)      Authenticate CLI (needs DOKPLOY_SERVER_URL)\n"
	@printf "  $(GREEN)dokploy-cli-verify$(RESET)    Verify CLI authentication\n"
	@printf "  $(GREEN)dokploy-cli-deploy$(RESET)    Deploy app via CLI (needs APP_ID)\n"
	@printf "  $(GREEN)dokploy-cli-env-push$(RESET)  Push .env.dokploy to remote app\n"
	@printf "\n$(BOLD)━━ Build ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━$(RESET)\n"
	@printf "  $(GREEN)build$(RESET)              Production build (Next.js)\n"
	@printf "\n$(BOLD)━━ Database ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━$(RESET)\n"
	@printf "  $(GREEN)db-migrate$(RESET)         Apply Supabase migrations\n"
	@printf "  $(GREEN)db-reset$(RESET)           Reset local Supabase DB\n"
--
	@printf "$(CYAN)▶ Building Next.js for production…$(RESET)\n"
	pnpm --filter carbon-bim build

# =============================================================================
# DOCKER — full stack (api + redis)
# =============================================================================
docker-up:
	@printf "$(CYAN)▶ Starting Suna services via Docker Compose…$(RESET)\n"
	cd $(BACKEND) && docker compose up -d
	@printf "$(GREEN)✔ Services running:$(RESET)\n"
	@printf "   API    → http://localhost:8000\n"
	@printf "   Redis  → localhost:6379\n"
	@printf "$(CYAN)   Start frontend separately: make dev-frontend$(RESET)\n"

docker-down:
	@printf "$(CYAN)▶ Stopping Docker services…$(RESET)\n"
	cd $(BACKEND) && docker compose down

docker-build:
	@printf "$(CYAN)▶ Rebuilding Docker images…$(RESET)\n"
	cd $(BACKEND) && docker compose build --no-cache

docker-logs:
	cd $(BACKEND) && docker compose logs -f

docker-logs-api:
	cd $(BACKEND) && docker compose logs -f api

docker-logs-redis:
	cd $(BACKEND) && docker compose logs -f redis

docker-ps:
	cd $(BACKEND) && docker compose ps

restart: docker-down docker-up

# =============================================================================
# DOKPLOY — production deployment
# =============================================================================
dokploy-setup:
	@if [ ! -f .env ]; then \
		cp .env.dokploy .env; \
		printf "$(GREEN)✔ Copied .env.dokploy → .env$(RESET)\n"; \
		printf "$(CYAN)  Edit .env and fill in REQUIRED values before running dokploy-up.$(RESET)\n"; \
	else \
		printf "$(CYAN).env already exists — skipping. Delete it first to reset.$(RESET)\n"; \
	fi

dokploy-build:
	@printf "$(CYAN)▶ Building Dokploy production images…$(RESET)\n"
	docker compose -f docker-compose.dokploy.yml build

dokploy-up:
	@printf "$(CYAN)▶ Starting Dokploy production stack…$(RESET)\n"
	docker compose -f docker-compose.dokploy.yml up -d
	@printf "$(GREEN)✔ Services running:$(RESET)\n"
	@printf "   Frontend  → http://localhost:3000\n"
	@printf "   Backend   → http://localhost:8000\n"

dokploy-down:
	@printf "$(CYAN)▶ Stopping Dokploy production stack…$(RESET)\n"
	docker compose -f docker-compose.dokploy.yml down

dokploy-logs:
	docker compose -f docker-compose.dokploy.yml logs -f

dokploy-validate:
	@printf "$(CYAN)▶ Validating docker-compose.dokploy.yml syntax…$(RESET)\n"
	docker compose -f docker-compose.dokploy.yml config --quiet && \
		printf "$(GREEN)✔ Compose file is valid$(RESET)\n"

# =============================================================================
# DOKPLOY CLI  — Remote deployment via @dokploy/cli
# Requires: DOKPLOY_SERVER_URL and DOKPLOY_TOKEN set in environment or .env.dokploy
# Usage:
#   export $(grep -v '^#' .env.dokploy | xargs)  # load vars
#   make dokploy-cli-auth
#   make dokploy-cli-deploy APP_ID=<applicationId>
# =============================================================================

# Load .env.dokploy vars into the shell if present
_DOKPLOY_ENV := $(shell test -f .env.dokploy && echo "set -a && . ./.env.dokploy && set +a &&")

dokploy-cli-install:
	@printf "$(CYAN)▶ Installing @dokploy/cli…$(RESET)\n"
	npm install -g @dokploy/cli
	@printf "$(GREEN)✔ dokploy CLI installed: $$(dokploy --version)$(RESET)\n"

dokploy-cli-auth:
	@test -n "$(DOKPLOY_SERVER_URL)" || (printf "$(BOLD)ERROR: DOKPLOY_SERVER_URL is not set.\n  Run: export DOKPLOY_SERVER_URL=https://your-panel-domain.com\n  Or load .env.dokploy first: export \$$(grep -v '^#' .env.dokploy | xargs)$(RESET)\n" && exit 1)
	@printf "$(CYAN)▶ Authenticating Dokploy CLI with $(DOKPLOY_SERVER_URL)…$(RESET)\n"
	dokploy authenticate \
		--url="$(DOKPLOY_SERVER_URL)" \
		--token="$(DOKPLOY_TOKEN)"
	@printf "$(GREEN)✔ Authenticated. Run 'make dokploy-cli-verify' to confirm.$(RESET)\n"

dokploy-cli-verify:
	@printf "$(CYAN)▶ Verifying Dokploy CLI token…$(RESET)\n"
	dokploy verify

dokploy-cli-projects:
	@printf "$(CYAN)▶ Listing Dokploy projects…$(RESET)\n"
	dokploy project list

dokploy-cli-deploy:
	@test -n "$(APP_ID)" || (printf "$(BOLD)ERROR: APP_ID is not set.\n  Usage: make dokploy-cli-deploy APP_ID=<applicationId>$(RESET)\n" && exit 1)
	@printf "$(CYAN)▶ Deploying application $(APP_ID)…$(RESET)\n"
	dokploy app deploy --applicationId="$(APP_ID)" --skipConfirm
	@printf "$(GREEN)✔ Deploy triggered for $(APP_ID)$(RESET)\n"

dokploy-cli-env-push:
	@test -n "$(APP_ID)" || (printf "$(BOLD)ERROR: APP_ID is not set.\n  Usage: make dokploy-cli-env-push APP_ID=<applicationId>$(RESET)\n" && exit 1)
	@printf "$(CYAN)▶ Pushing .env.dokploy to application $(APP_ID)…$(RESET)\n"
	dokploy env push .env.dokploy
	@printf "$(GREEN)✔ Environment variables pushed$(RESET)\n"

# Combined: Docker backend + local frontend dev
start: docker-up dev-frontend

stop: docker-down

# =============================================================================
# DATABASE
# =============================================================================
db-migrate:
<exited with exit code 0>
```

**view the file at /teamspace/studios/this_studio/suna-bim-agent/cloudflare/worker.ts.**

/teamspace/studios/this_studio/suna-bim-agent/cloudflare/worker.ts

<details>
<summary>52 lines</summary>

```
1. // Simple proxy Worker: forwards all requests to the Dokploy backend VM
2. const BACKEND_URL = "http://20.55.21.69:40";
3. 
4. export default {
5.   async fetch(request: Request): Promise<Response> {
6.     const url = new URL(request.url);
7. 
8.     // Handle CORS preflight
9.     if (request.method === "OPTIONS") {
10.       return new Response(null, {
11.         headers: {
12.           "Access-Control-Allow-Origin": "*",
13.           "Access-Control-Allow-Methods": "GET, POST, PUT, DELETE, PATCH, OPTIONS",
14.           "Access-Control-Allow-Headers": "Content-Type, Authorization, x-api-key",
15.           "Access-Control-Max-Age": "86400",
16.         },
17.       });
18.     }
19. 
20.     // Proxy the request to the Dokploy backend
21.     const backendUrl = `${BACKEND_URL}${url.pathname}${url.search}`;
22.     const proxyRequest = new Request(backendUrl, {
23.       method: request.method,
24.       headers: request.headers,
25.       body: request.method !== "GET" && request.method !== "HEAD" ? request.body : undefined,
26.     });
27. 
28.     let response: Response;
29.     try {
30.       response = await fetch(proxyRequest);
31.     } catch (err) {
32.       return new Response(JSON.stringify({ error: "Backend unavailable", detail: String(err) }), {
33.         status: 503,
34.         headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json", "Access-Control-Allow-Origin": "*" },
35.       });
36.     }
37. 
38.     // Forward the response with CORS headers added
39.     const headers = new Headers(response.headers);
40.     headers.set("Access-Control-Allow-Origin", "*");
41.     headers.set("Access-Control-Allow-Methods", "GET, POST, PUT, DELETE, PATCH, OPTIONS");
42.     headers.set("Access-Control-Allow-Headers", "Content-Type, Authorization, x-api-key");
43. 
44.     return new Response(response.body, {
45.       status: response.status,
46.       statusText: response.statusText,
47.       headers,
48.     });
49.   },
50. } satisfies ExportedHandler;
51. 
52.
```
